writeup: New Year's Day 2026 found the Buffalo Bills deep in Week 18 prep mode, with the team holding practice at One Bills Drive as they readied for Sunday's season finale against the New York Jets — almost certainly the last-ever game at Highmark Stadium. The biggest storyline heading into the holiday week was Josh Allen's lingering right foot injury, with coach Sean McDermott taking a cautious approach and keeping his star QB off the practice field while stressing he was trending better than the week prior. Meanwhile, the Bills' playoff seeding remained fluid: a win over the lowly Jets, combined with the right results elsewhere, could lift Buffalo from the No. 7 seed all the way to No. 5 or 6 in the AFC bracket. Analysts and beat writers spent the day publishing previews, predictions, and rooting-guide pieces, all underpinned by the emotional backdrop of bidding farewell to 'The Ralph' — a stadium that had housed Bills Mafia for 52 seasons.
